design_philosophy.core_loop: Real-time combat with weapon charge mechanic + exploration of themed realms + alchemy formula discovery and experimentation + NPC trading chains
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Resource management (ingredients, currency conversion across realms) vs. combat execution (charge-based damage timing) vs. exploration risk (seeking out rare formulas from hidden alchemists)
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Adventuring boy and shapeshifting dog traversing a world spanning prehistory through futuristic civilization, mastering evolving weaponry and magical alchemy
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Deliberate departure from Secret of Mana formula despite mechanical similarity; alchemy requires paired ingredients sourced from environment (dog sniffing mechanic) rather than MP consumption; weapon progression resets per realm (forcing remastery); currency conversion and barter trading as core progression gates; divisive design that prioritized American development creativity over Japanese RPG expectations

difficulty.checkpointing: Town/cave refuge recovery of hit points; no explicit checkpoint system documented
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Real-time combat requires execution skill (charge gauge management); exploration for rare formulas and optimal ingredient sourcing provides strategic depth; weapon choice and alchemy preparation affect combat viability; no difficulty selection documented

progression.upgrade_system: Weapon skill levels increase (max 3) via repeated use, unlocking charged attacks and range/power bonuses; weapon variants provide stat resets forcing re-leveling in each realm (Prehistoria → Antiqua → Gothica → Omnitopia); alchemy formula levels enhance effectiveness with use; character level-up via experience increases stats (strength, evasion, etc.)
progression.unlock_mechanism: Alchemy formulas gated by NPC discovery (some hidden in remote areas); realm progression is linear; weapons naturally upgrade via story (bazooka acquired late game); ingredient availability tied to environment/realm
replayability_hooks: Multiple alchemy formulas with trading chains encourage experimentation
Hidden alchemists with rare formulas reward exploration
Ingredient sourcing via dog sniffing creates environmental puzzle-solving
Marketplace bartering in Antiqua and Gothica adds trading depth

world_building_techniques: Four distinct historical/conceptual realms: Prehistoria, Antiqua (classical antiquity), Gothica (Middle Ages), Omnitopia (imaginative future)
Currency and trade goods shift per realm (talons → gemstones → gold → credits), reinforcing world distinctiveness
NPC alchemists embedded in world serve dual narrative/mechanical role
Shapeshifting dog companion provides thematic variation
Environment-specific alchemy ingredients (wax/limestone/clay in primitive realms; ethanol/dry ice in advanced realms)

faction_indicators: Alchemist NPCs as knowledge-gatekeepers across realms
Realm-specific merchant cultures (Antiqua marketplace traders, Gothica merchants)
The boy and dog shapeshifting relationship as outsider pairing
danger_signals: Charge gauge depletion during combat creates vulnerability windows
Ingredient scarcity in certain realms forces strategic planning
Weapon skill resets at realm transitions temporarily reduce combat power
environmental_storytelling: Realm-specific vegetation and rock types require tool-specific weapons
Ingredient locations sniffed by dog suggest environmental resource ecology
Town/cave refuge placement structures traversal safety
Currency shrines and conversion NPCs mark cultural boundaries
